title: The Abandoned Channel Archive

features.name: dry riverbed stones
features.desc-on-touch: the channel remembers its water as a presence, not an absence; touch the smooth stone and feel the ghost current

features.name: archive ledge along bank
features.desc-on-touch: files deposited in the long eddy where the current slowed; waterlogged folders containing names of towns the water visited

features.name: eddy pool mirror
features.desc-on-touch: still water that reflects things the current left behind; you see your own reading-image returned to you

features.name: bend where flow stopped
features.desc-on-touch: the geometry of reluctance; the channel turns back on itself in a shape that means: the water chose another path

reverie.title: REVERIE — the tributary's last broadcast
narr: rain is falling and you wade in a channel that hasn't
held water in three seasons; she's recording on her handheld,
documenting the absence. you realize: this is how archives
grow—by learning to preserve what chose to leave, what

inscription: water never truly leaves a channel; it only changes where it wants to be heard

file.name: Channel_Discontinuation_Record_1987.txt
file.note: final measurements taken the year the tributary stopped flowing; coordinate readings for a path the water no longer takes

exits.label: back toward the side channel where memory still holds
toward sediments that haven't settled yet
toward the delta where all forgotten channels converge at last
